title: chaerophyllum cicutaria var. alpestre
intro: Chaerophyllum cicutaria var. alpestre, also known as alpine chervil, is a plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its expectorant, diuretic, and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful for treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of alpine chervil include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide antioxidant properties. Alpine chervil can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
